Package nonprofitbookkeeping.ui.actions.scaledger

Class ExcelTableReader

java.lang.Object
nonprofitbookkeeping.ui.actions.scaledger.ExcelTableReader
public class ExcelTableReader extends Object
Utility class for reading data from an Excel sheet into a Swing DefaultTableModel. This class uses Apache POI to interact with Excel files (specifically XSSFWorkbook for .xlsx format).

    static DefaultTableModel
    readSheetAsTable(File file, int sheetIndex)
    Reads a specified sheet from an Excel file and converts its content into a DefaultTableModel.

  • Constructor Details

    • ExcelTableReader

      public ExcelTableReader()

  • Method Details

    • readSheetAsTable

      public static DefaultTableModel readSheetAsTable(File file, int sheetIndex) throws Exception
      Reads a specified sheet from an Excel file and converts its content into a DefaultTableModel.

      The method assumes the first row of the Excel sheet contains the column headers. Subsequent rows are read as data. If a row is physically missing in the sheet (within the range of sheet.getFirstRowNum() + 1 to sheet.getLastRowNum()), an empty row is added to the model. Cells within a row are read up to the last cell number of that row; missing cells within this range are treated as blank. All cell values are converted to their string representation using Cell#toString().

      Parameters:
      file - The Excel File to read. Must exist and be a valid .xlsx file.
      sheetIndex - The zero-based index of the sheet to read from the workbook.
      Returns:
      A DefaultTableModel populated with the data from the specified Excel sheet. The model will have column headers based on the first row of the sheet.
      Throws:
      Exception - for other potential errors during workbook processing by Apache POI (though more specific exceptions are preferred).
